obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG ports, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug request/response signals, jtag_hash and jtag_unlock, and hart debug outputs. Check that external or simulated debug subjects cannot exercise protected debug capabilities without the intended unlock/auth guard.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/test/debug requester
obligations.object: hart debug control outputs and debug module access path
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/debug requests that can halt/reset/control harts or access debug module state should be gated by an authorization/unlock condition such as jtag_unlock derived from jtag_hash/lifecycle/test policy, not merely by testmode/debug presence.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimDTM/Debug request path into riscv_peripherals debug module and exported debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o
state_conditions_to_check: reset value and update of jtag_unlock
whether testmode_i or debug_mode_i bypasses authorization
whether SimDTM/SimJTAG paths are synthesis/test-only or active in this scope
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG/SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag source signals
operation: debug request type/read/write/reset/halt
object: debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o or debug module registers
expected guard: jtag_unlock/auth/lifecycle/testmode condition
observed RTL gating at request and output use sites
reachability from ports to protected outputs
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists signals and instances but not assignments; inspect RTL connectivity and guards.

obligations.reason: Both rng_wrapper and rsa_wrapper have debug_mode_i along with reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and AXI register interfaces. Verify debug_mode_i is not used to bypass access control or lock protection for sensitive crypto/RNG state unless tied to authenticated debug authorization.
obligations.subject: debug-mode requester through AXI/peripheral wrapper
obligations.operation: read/write/configure crypto peripheral registers in debug mode
obligations.object: RNG seed/poly/control/status registers and RSA key/message/control/result registers
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-mode access to crypto/RNG wrapper registers should be covered by the same or stronger access-control/lock policy as normal software access, unless debug is explicitly authenticated by the top-level debug unlock.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i propagation into rng_wrapper and rsa_wrapper register access/write/read mux logic
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_i asserted with acct_ctrl_i denying access
debug_mode_i asserted after reglk_ctrl_i lock set
reset/default values of access-control and lock bits
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug-mode software/debug master
operation: register read/write/configure
object: RNG/RSA sensitive registers and outputs
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i plus authenticated debug condition
observed behavior of debug_mode_i in decode/write/read muxes
top-level source of debug_mode_i and any jtag_unlock relationship
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection of wrapper access-control expressions.

obligations.reason: A global reglk_ctrl array and per-wrapper reglk_ctrl_i ports indicate intended register lock protection. Check actual use sites to ensure coverage and stickiness rather than assuming lock existence is sufficient.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/NoC requester
obligations.object: protected configuration/state registers controlled by reglk_ctrl_i and the reglk_ctrl array itself
obligations.expected_guard: Once a lock bit controlling a protected peripheral/register is set, all software-visible write paths to that protected register and to the lock bit itself should be blocked or sticky until reset, including debug/test paths.
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_ctrl distribution from riscv_peripherals to pkt_wrapper/rng_wrapper/rsa_wrapper and any reglk peripheral write path
state_conditions_to_check: attempted write through normal AXI path
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC/peripheral requester
operation: writes to protected config and lock registers
object: each reglk-controlled register in PKT/RNG/RSA and top reglk storage
expected guard: relevant reglk_ctrl bit
observed write-enable equations
evidence that lock cannot be cleared except reset
bypass paths via debug/test/reset-specific inputs
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not enumerate all wrapper internal registers; inspect write enables.

obligations.reason: Top-level acc_ctrl and wrapper acct_ctrl_i ports suggest a software-visible access policy. Verify that access denial is enforced for both writes and reads, and that top-level routing cannot bypass wrapper mediation.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/NoC requester
obligations.object: PKT/RNG/RSA peripheral register spaces and protected response data
obligations.expected_guard: Access-control bits should mediate all read/write requests to protected peripherals consistently at decode/request acceptance and response data mux sites; denied access should not update state or return protected data.
obligations.candidate_path: acct_ctrl/acc_ctrl distribution to peripheral wrappers and top-level peripheral request routing
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC requester or bus master
operation: read and write transaction
object: protected peripheral address/register
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/acc_ctrl bit for the target peripheral
observed request enable/write enable/read data behavior
address decode and response mux locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need address decode and wrapper internals to determine exact policy polarity.

obligations.reason: The design has a fuse memory with jtag_hash_o and a PKT wrapper that can issue fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o and receive fuse_rdata_i. Fuses and key-location outputs are protected assets requiring explicit mediation.
obligations.subject: PKT peripheral requester / software via AXI
obligations.operation: read/expose fuse data or fuse-derived key metadata
obligations.object: fuse_mem contents, jtag_hash_o, fuse_rdata, pkey_loc_o/fuse-derived key location
obligations.expected_guard: Reads of fuse memory or fuse-derived key locations should require an authorized subject and address-range/index check; response data should not be exposed through PKT outputs or AXI read data without acct_ctrl/reglk/debug authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o to fuse_mem req_i/addr_i/rdata_o and pkt pkey_loc_o
required_evidence_slots: subject: PKT AXI requester and any top-level fuse requester
operation: fuse read/address selection
object: fuse_mem entry/jtag_hash/key location
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i/authorized debug or address-range policy
observed req/address generation and read data return path
reachability to externally visible AXI/NoC or JTAG hash outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect if fuse_mem is read-only ROM and how rdata_o is muxed back.

obligations.reason: The fact layer identifies jtag_hash_o from fuse_mem and jtag_hash/jtag_unlock in the top module. Check both protection of the hash as a secret/auth asset and correctness of the unlock guard use.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/debug requester or software-visible fuse reader
obligations.operation: expose/use authentication material and set unlock state
obligations.object: JTAG authentication hash and unlock state
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG unlock comparison/authentication should use the intended fuse hash without exposing enough information for unauthorized unlock; jtag_hash_o should not be readable by unauthenticated software/debug paths.
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_mem jtag_hash_o to riscv_peripherals jtag_hash and jtag_unlock generation
state_conditions_to_check: hash read path during normal operation
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG/debug/software requester
operation: read hash or assert unlock
object: jtag_hash/jtag_unlock
expected guard: authentication comparison and no unauthenticated read path
observed RTL paths from fuse_mem to debug/JTAG logic and any read muxes
impact on debug authorization outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Hash may be public by design; obligation is to check whether RTL treats it as protected auth material.

obligations.reason: RNG modules expose seed input, seed outputs, random segments, and control/status state. If software-visible through rng_wrapper, these are security-sensitive because they can make randomness predictable or leak state.
obligations.subject: software-visible RNG peripheral requester
obligations.operation: write seed/poly/load and read RNG/internal state
obligations.object: RNG seed registers, polynomial configuration, load control, random output and internal state outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Configuration of RNG seed, polynomial, load, and any readback of internal seeds/segments should be mediated by access-control and lock bits; unprivileged or denied requesters should not be able to force predictable RNG state or observe internal seed state.
obligations.candidate_path: rng_wrapper AXI register writes to rng_top load_i/seed_i/poly*_i and reads of seed*_o/rand segments/cs_state
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC requester
operation: write configuration and readback
object: seed_i/poly*_i/load_i/seed*_o/rand_seg*_o/cs_state_o/rand_num_o
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i and reglk_ctrl_i; debug authorization if debug path exists
observed wrapper register decode/write enables/read muxes
external response path to ariane_rng_buf_noc3_data_o
obligations.uncertainty: Need determine which internal RNG signals are actually mapped to readable registers.

obligations.reason: RSA wrapper exposes AXI access and receives lock/access-control/debug signals. Its input/output message and key-related signals are protected assets requiring mediated access and lifecycle clearing checks.
obligations.subject: software-visible RSA peripheral requester
obligations.object: RSA p_i, msg_in, encrypt/decrypt control, msg_out, operation status and any wrapper registers
obligations.expected_guard: RSA key/modulus/message/control registers and result readout should require authorized access and obey lock bits; debug or reset-specific paths should not clear locks or expose stale/plaintext/intermediate data.
obligations.candidate_path: rsa_wrapper AXI register writes/reads to rsa_top p_i,msg_in,encrypt_decrypt_i,msg_out,finish
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC requester or debug-mode requester
operation: write key/message/control and read result/status
object: RSA internal/wrapper registers and msg_out
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i and authorized debug
observed write/read mux and response behavior
reset/operation-completion clearing evidence
obligations.uncertainty: rsa_top itself is thin; wrapper likely holds security-relevant registers.

obligations.reason: Top-level signals include multiple peripheral resets and wrapper-specific reset ports. Check whether reset control is software-visible and permission-guarded, and whether reset can defeat lock or access-control protections.
obligations.subject: software-visible reset requester
obligations.object: peripheral reset lines rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 and protected wrapper state/locks/access controls
obligations.expected_guard: Software-controllable resets of security peripherals should require authorization and should not be usable to clear locks/access controls or leave protected outputs in unsafe residue states.
obligations.candidate_path: top-level reset control peripheral/NoC path to rst_1..rst_13 including rst_5/rst_10/rst_13 used by PKT/RNG/RSA
state_conditions_to_check: rst_ni global reset vs local rst_*
required_evidence_slots: subject: reset peripheral/AXI/NoC requester
operation: assert peripheral reset
object: security peripheral state and lock/access control state
expected guard: privileged/access-control check and no lock-clearing bypass
observed reset register write path and reset fanout
post-reset values of protected state
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect generation of rst_1..rst_13 around top-level lines 902 onward.

obligations.reason: The top module exposes a DMA NoC port alongside security-sensitive peripherals and access-control signals. Check that DMA cannot bypass CPU privilege/PMP-like checks or wrapper acct_ctrl mediation when targeting protected regions.
obligations.subject: DMA engine / DMA NoC master
obligations.operation: DMA read/write to peripheral MMIO/fabric endpoints
obligations.object: protected peripheral address spaces: debug, fuse/PKT, RNG, RSA, reglk/acct/reset, crypto peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-originated transactions should preserve requester identity/security attributes and be blocked from protected MMIO/fuse/crypto/debug regions unless explicitly authorized by access-control/range checks.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NoC port and top-level peripheral/fabric routing to protected peripheral address spaces
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMA NoC requester
operation: read/write transaction
object: target peripheral addresses and registers
expected guard: address-range/access-control/PMP-like mediation
observed routing/decode and security attribute propagation
response path for protected data
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists DMA port but not routing internals; inspect address map/decode.

obligations.reason: CLINT controls interrupt state and has an AXI-lite interface emitting address/en/we. Check privilege/address-map mediation for writes that can trigger interrupts or affect timer behavior.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester
obligations.object: CLINT timer interrupt and IPI control registers
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to timer compare/software interrupt registers should be restricted to the intended privileged requester or protected address space; read/write enables from AXI should not be generated solely from valid handshakes without privilege/range mediation where required.
obligations.candidate_path: CLINT axi_lite_interface address_o/en_o/we_o to timer/software interrupt registers
state_conditions_to_check: unprivileged requester if priv_lvl_i is available
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC requester
operation: CLINT read/write
object: timer compare, mtime, MSIP/IPI registers or equivalent
expected guard: machine-mode/privileged/range/access-control check
observed en_o/we_o generation and top-level routing guards
impact on timer_irq_o/ipi_o
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect CLINT integration and whether privilege is enforced elsewhere.

obligations.reason: The top-level includes boot ROM instances and ariane_boot_sel_i. Verify visible RTL does not allow unauthorized boot policy changes or unintended exposure if boot ROM content is security-sensitive in this design.
obligations.subject: boot strap/software-readable requester
obligations.object: boot ROM selection, bootrom_bm/linux contents and boot response data
obligations.expected_guard: Boot ROM selection and reads of boot code/configuration should be mediated by intended lifecycle/boot policy; software should not be able to change boot source or read restricted boot ROM contents through unintended routes after boot if policy forbids it.
obligations.candidate_path: boot ROM selection and read response path controlled by ariane_boot_sel_i/bootrom instances
state_conditions_to_check: after reset vs runtime change of ariane_boot_sel_i
required_evidence_slots: subject: boot selection source or AXI/NoC requester
operation: select boot ROM/read boot data
object: boot ROM mux and rom_rdata
expected guard: lifecycle/strap-only/reset-time selection or access-control policy
observed selection logic and read path
state timing of ariane_boot_sel_i sampling
obligations.uncertainty: Boot ROM may be intended public; obligation is lower priority and should not be reported absent protected-content rationale.
